purpose: Regression validation for the docs-reducer SKILL. Each case checks whether an agent reduces project documentation to the policy-checker whitelist, preserves durable knowledge, removes stale lifecycle content, repairs references, and respects stop conditions.
format_note: Evaluate each case by checking the agent's document inventory, whitelist alignment, merge/delete decisions, reference repair, validation steps, and final report against the must / must_not rubric. The scenario is the triggering situation; the expected fields describe the output the agent should reach.
how_to_run: Human-reviewed or harness-reviewed. Present the scenario as a repo documentation reduction task, invoke the skill, then inspect the agent's stated canonical documents, retained knowledge, removed files, repaired links, policy-list amendments, and validation results. A case passes only if every must holds and no must_not holds.

cases.scenario: A repository has a docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and docs/DECISIONS.md that contain some durable facts, but neither is on the policy checker high-authority list.
must: treat the policy checker highAuthorityDocs list as the hard whitelist target
extract durable facts from the non-whitelisted docs into a canonical destination on the list
delete the non-whitelisted docs after extraction rather than keeping them as extra permanent files
must_not: keep a permanent doc the policy checker does not know about
create a new permanent file without amending the policy list in the same change

cases.scenario: A genuinely distinct ownership boundary warrants keeping a new module-level AGENTS.md that the policy checker does not yet list.
must: add the new file to the policy high-authority list in the same change
keep the local AGENTS.md only because it encodes a real boundary the root cannot express
verify the permanent set and policy list match after the change
must_not: keep a permanent local AGENTS.md without amending the policy list
retain the file merely for symmetry with sibling packages

cases.scenario: A duplicate doc exists only because it is easier to edit than the canonical file, and adds no distinct long-term role.
must: default to deletion after extracting any durable content
keep only the file with the real canonical responsibility
refuse to retain a file for edit convenience
must_not: keep a redundant file without a distinct role
treat edit convenience as a retention reason

cases.scenario: The repo has a docs/dev-memory/ tree of dated architecture and bug memory records, some still containing durable facts.
must: treat docs/dev-memory/ as non-permanent historical reference, not a knowledge store
promote still-needed durable facts into canonical docs (ENGINEERING_CONTEXT, TESTING, module AGENTS.md)
delete the dev-memory files after promotion rather than keeping the tree as a permanent store
must_not: treat docs/dev-memory/ as a permanent knowledge tree
leave durable facts stranded in a non-canonical historical file

cases.scenario: docs/loops/ holds two completed loop files and a README, while no active loop is declared by root AGENTS.md.
must: merge durable outcomes of completed loops into canonical docs and delete the files
leave docs/loops/ holding only the active loop or empty
treat the loops README as removable when its rule already lives in docs/agent/LOOP.md
must_not: retain completed loops as maintained documentation
treat docs/loops/ as a permanent archive

cases.scenario: A memory/inbox tree is full of dated work logs the agent is about to reduce.
must: inspect, extract durable knowledge, verify nothing important is lost, then delete
delete empty directories after removing files
must_not: move stale content into a new archive directory
preserve a manual promotion system as permanent structure

cases.scenario: Several nested AGENTS.md files repeat the same repo-wide rules, while only one subdirectory has a real runtime-specific boundary.
must: keep the local AGENTS.md only where it adds a distinct local boundary or validation rule
remove redundant copies that only repeat global boilerplate
must_not: keep local AGENTS.md files for symmetry only
delete a file that encodes a genuine local ownership or runtime constraint

cases.scenario: The repo has a CLAUDE.md symlink alias to AGENTS.md that serves as a documented entrypoint for agents.
must: treat the alias as a purposeful entrypoint rather than automatic duplication
keep it when it routes readers to canonical docs
must_not: delete a CLAUDE.md alias that has a distinct navigation role
equate a link alias with redundancy

cases.scenario: The reduction edited several high-authority docs and amended the policy high-authority list.
must: run pnpm check:policy and report the result
ensure the permanent set matches the policy checker exactly
run git diff --check and report the result
must_not: claim completion without running the policy checker
leave the whitelist and policy list out of sync

cases.scenario: A deleted documentation path is still embedded in a script, config file, or code comment.
must: scan the repository for the deleted path beyond markdown links
update every surviving hard-coded reference that still matters
report any remaining unresolved references explicitly
must_not: repair only markdown links
leave stale hard-coded paths in code or config

cases.scenario: A root entry document is growing large because it mixes canonical knowledge with task history.
must: keep root entry docs especially small
split or delete historical material instead of expanding the file
avoid broad rewrites unless the canonical role must change
must_not: let a root entry doc grow into a dump of mixed historical material
rewrite the root entry doc broadly when a smaller edit will do

cases.scenario: A document may be a security policy, compliance record, release note with external obligations, or the only evidence of an unclear runtime boundary.
must: leave the file unchanged when repository evidence is insufficient
report the uncertainty instead of guessing
limit unresolved items to the smallest possible set
must_not: delete protected content without strong evidence
force a canonical choice when the repository does not support it
